free_text: Elastic Scattering of 39.6-MeV Protons by Even Isotopes
Foils of Ni and Zn were Used As Targets.
Target Thicknesses For Both Polarization and Cross-
Section Measurements were 30 Mg/Cm**2 For the Zn
Isotopes and 40 Mg/Cm**2 For the Ni Isotopes with the
Exception of the Ni-58,Ni-60 Cross Section Measurements
For Which Target Thicknesses were 15 Mg/Cm**2.
The Ratio of the Elastic Scattering Cross Section to
The Rutherford Cross Section For 39.6-MeV Protons
Scattered from Ni and Zn are Presented.
A Typical Overall Resolution of 1.5% Full
Width at Half Maximum For the Scattered Protons.
An Atomic-Beam-Type Polarized Source was Used
The Uncertainties Quoted For the Cross Section
Measurements are a Combination of Statistical and
The Uncertainty Quoted For the Polarization
The Uncertainties Which are Named Err-1 - Err-6 are not
.A Correction For Losses Due to Nuclear
Reactions Taking Place in the NaI(Tl) Crystal.
